text.title: Strategy Analysis — BABOK KA-6 Four Tasks
text.summary: Four KA-6 tasks with strict input/output contracts, technique selection guidance, and known limitations of purely procedural execution.

rule.statement: Produce a current-state artifact covering six areas: business processes, organization structure, technology landscape, people and skills, data and information, external factors. Each area row must carry evidence_url and interviewed[] — no area may be populated from assumptions alone.
rule.rationale: Agents that take the README's linear arrow at face value skip the iteration loop; current-state is not a one-sitting output but a validated artifact that may require multiple elicitation rounds.

rule.statement: Choose techniques explicitly and record the choice: SWOT (engagement cadence tool), PESTLE (macro-environment), capability mapping (what the org can do), value-chain analysis (where value is created), business model canvas (how value is captured). Justify the choice against the question being answered.
rule.rationale: Agents default to SWOT and PESTLE without justification; naming better-fitting techniques (capability mapping, value-chain) produces richer current-state evidence.

rule.statement: Every goal in the future state must carry all six fields: metric, baseline, baseline_date, target, target_date, owner. Reject any goal missing a field or where baseline equals target. No "TBD" values — flag and re-elicit.
rule.rationale: Goals with missing baselines cannot generate a gap analysis; goals where baseline equals target are hallucinated progress.

rule.statement: Future state must be signed off by the exec sponsor before risk assessment and strategy work begin.
rule.rationale: Risk assessment and strategy options are meaningless without a locked future state; proceeding without sign-off wastes subsequent work when the target changes.

rule.statement: Produce two risk registers: risks of making the change AND risks of not making the change. Both are required by BABOK KA-6; an artifact with only one register is incomplete.
rule.rationale: The "do nothing" risk register provides the baseline against which the change strategy is justified; omitting it makes the recommendation unconditional.

rule.statement: Present at least three options (build, buy, partner, modify existing, status-quo / do nothing), each with cost-band, time-band, risk-rating, and capability-fit. Never omit the status-quo option — it is the leadership baseline.
rule.rationale: Agents skip the status-quo option because it "feels weak"; without it the recommendation looks unconditional and cannot be challenged fairly.

rule.statement: A change strategy produces a strategy memo, not a plan. A memo has options, a recommendation, and a rationale. Gantt charts and sprint plans are downstream artifacts that belong in implementation-plan.md, not in the strategy.
rule.rationale: Agents confuse strategy with plan; a 30-page implementation roadmap where a 3-page strategy memo was needed delays decisions by burying the recommendation.

antipattern.description: Solution-shaped business need: "we need a CRM" presented as the business need.
antipattern.reason: The framer must reject anything not outcome-shaped; require the stakeholder to state the problem the CRM solves before accepting the request.

antipattern.description: Stale current-state artifact used without freshness check.
antipattern.reason: An agent should check last_validated_at and refuse to proceed if the artifact is older than 90 days; stale context produces gap analysis against a changed baseline.

antipattern.description: Trace decay: requirements created after KA-6 have no traces_to the originating goal.
antipattern.reason: Pre-commit hook rejects orphan requirements; every requirement must carry a traces_to: GOAL-XX frontmatter entry.

text.reference: Business need statement with strategic alignment and measurable success metrics
Gap analysis table across process, technology, people, and data dimensions
